basics.name: Rahil Sharma
basics.label: Systems Researcher
basics.summary: I think of myself as a systems researcher. I am interested in why systems and protocols are shaped the way they are, and what their architectural choices enable, constrain, prevent, or imply. My work spans three connected areas: systems and protocol design, reasoning about trade-offs under constraints (where security, privacy, scalability, and efficiency pull against each other), and the sociotechnical impact of systems. I bring 5+ years of combined industry, research, and teaching experience across enterprise data systems, privacy-preserving computation, explainable AI, and distributed systems. Currently seeking PhD opportunities in privacy-preserving systems, trustworthy AI, and sociotechnical computing.

work.name: Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI)
work.position: Research Assistant — CSY Group (Security and Machine Learning)
work.summary: Thesis research and implementation of privacy-preserving cryptographic protocols.
highlights: Built distributed Python and gRPC implementations of competing privacy-preserving cryptographic protocols, incorporating encrypted communication, persistent storage, configurable execution workflows, and systematic correctness testing.
Translated formal theoretical protocol specifications into executable systems, resolving ambiguities between prior research and practical implementation constraints.
Designed and ran a sensitivity study across varying batch sizes and filter capacities to evaluate real-world system behaviour and characterise crossover points between protocols.
Developed a decision framework for protocol selection based on observed performance trends and deployment parameters.
Collaborated with researchers and supervisors on architecture decisions, code reviews, experimental design, and technical documentation.

work.name: Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
work.summary: Graduate teaching assistant across machine learning, robotics, NLP, and systems engineering courses.
highlights: Mentored students across machine learning, robotics, swarm intelligence, text mining, and systems engineering projects.
Led lab sessions, guided experiment design, supported debugging of complex software systems, and supervised project development.
Supervised a cohort of 6 bachelor students through the Software Asset Management thesis module at VU Amsterdam.
Also served as TA for the Creative Technology (CreaTe) programme at the University of Twente across AI, programming, and hardware modules.
Contributed to grading frameworks, evaluation criteria, and assessment materials.

work.name: Precisely
work.position: Software Support Engineer I — Data Integration
work.summary: Enterprise data integration engineering across cloud, distributed, and legacy environments.
highlights: Built, configured, and maintained enterprise batch and real-time data pipelines using Precisely Connect CDC, ETL, and Connect for Big Data across cloud, distributed, and legacy environments.
Onboarded enterprise customers from proof of concept through production go-live, validating connectivity, source-to-target mappings, transformation logic, and operational reliability.
Developed source-to-target mappings, schema configurations, and transformation logic using SQL, dbt, and DTL, with validation and reconciliation checks for data quality issues.
Used Terraform, GitHub, and Azure DevOps within established CI/CD workflows to support repeatable cloud-platform changes, testing, and controlled production releases.
Built reusable integration patterns across Databricks, Spark, Kafka, Snowflake, and cloud platforms enabling downstream engineering and analytics teams to consume reliable data.
Monitored business-critical pipelines and resolved failures, replication latency, connectivity issues, and data inconsistencies across ingestion, transformation, and storage layers.
Contributed expertise to an internal AI/LLM pilot for support engineers, shaping retrieval workflows and use cases around recurring production issues.

publications.name: OBFI: Implementation, Empirical Analysis, and Crossover Characterization of Competing Protocols
publications.summary: First empirical implementation and comparison of two cryptographic protocols for oblivious Bloom filter insertion. Supervised by Dr. M. van Dijk, CWI Amsterdam. Manuscript in preparation.

publications.name: Auditable Financial Fraud Detection: Graph Features and Agentic Investigation
publications.summary: End-to-end fraud detection pipeline with graph-based features, AI-assisted investigation, and confidence-based human-in-the-loop governance.

projects.name: OBFI: Implementation, Empirical Analysis, and Crossover Characterization of Competing Protocols
projects.summary: Distributed implementation and empirical comparison of two cryptographic protocols for privacy-preserving computation. MSc thesis, CWI Amsterdam.

projects.name: Auditable Financial Fraud Detection: Graph Features & Agentic Investigation
projects.summary: End-to-end fraud detection pipeline on 6.3M+ transactions with graph features, agentic investigation, and human-in-the-loop governance.

projects.name: Explainable AI Framework for Privacy-Aware Feature Attribution
projects.summary: Privacy-aware feature selection combining SHAP values and knowledge graphs, aligned with GDPR data minimization principles.

projects.name: Adaptive AI Interaction & Reasoning Platform
projects.summary: Multi-model RAG platform supporting structured evaluation of reasoning quality across LLaMA, Qwen, and GPT model families.

projects.name: Energy-Performance Trade-offs of Java Garbage Collection Strategies
projects.summary: Quantitative empirical study comparing GC strategies under controlled workloads using ANOVA and Tukey HSD statistical analysis.

projects.name: Reactivity-Reflectivity Vector Framework
projects.summary: Computational framework studying political polarization and media framing using large-scale YouTube transcript and comment data.
